The commission is authorized to contract for professional
services in proceeding with its responsibilities.
Recently, a proposal has been made to the commission which is
very unique in my experience, and with which I have great
concerns. Counsel for the commission has opined that there is no
conflict of interest inherent in the proposal. However, before
considering the matter, f am requesting your opinion as to the
legality and propriety of the proposal.
Generally, two individuals who wish to professionally
represent the commission have formed a separate non-profit
foundation, and will serve as the foundation's fund-raisers. 
The
foundation would accept gifts in support of development of a super
speed train and, would in turn, donate funds to the commission.
Originally, the proposal specified that those funds would be
used to pay for representation for the commission. Now, however,
under alternative 2 for "Lobbyist Compensation" of the proposed

sections.question: Please advise as to the relationship between the lobbyists, the foundation for which they intend to raise money, and the commission.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asks whether a proposed arrangement where two individuals would represent a transportation commission while simultaneously serving as fund-raisers for a separate non-profit foundation that would donate funds back to the commission to pay for that representation creates conflicts of interest or legal/propriety issues.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No CONCLUSION section exists. The QUESTION section is clearly stated in the closing paragraph. Document contains some OCR artifacts and incomplete text but the core question is discernible.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Please advise as to the relationship between the lobbyists, the foundation for which they intend to raise money, and the commission.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request from the California Assembly Committee on Transportation chairman to the FPPC asking for an opinion on whether a proposed arrangement involving lobbyists, a non-profit foundation, and commission compensation raises conflict of interest concerns.
